description_long: This object is a straight-bladed sword with a dark, metallic blade that tapers to a sharp point. The blade is long and narrow, with a consistent width along most of its length before narrowing at the tip. The crossguard is simple and slightly curved, made of a darker metal or alloy, providing protection for the hand. The handle is cylindrical and appears to be made of a different material, possibly wood or metal, with a subtle end cap or pommel at the base. The overall design is utilitarian, with no visible ornamentation or decorative features. The sword's construction suggests it is designed for practical use rather than ceremonial display.
description: A straight, dark-bladed sword with a simple crossguard and cylindrical handle, designed for practical use. The object has a utilitarian appearance with no visible ornamentation.
description_view_0: Front view showing the full length of the straight blade, crossguard, and handle.
description_view_1: Side view revealing the thin profile of the blade and the narrowness of the handle.
description_view_2: Rear view, nearly identical to the front, displaying the blade, crossguard, and handle.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, again showing the slim profile of the blade and handle.
attribute_handle_design: straight cylindrical handle, likely wood or metal, plain with a subtle end cap
